seo.title: Building Content Generation with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation apps with Vercel AI SDK enables fast, streaming UIs for LLM-powered creation, but risks like hallucinations and brand inconsistency can damage credibility. Vex provides runtime verification to enforce brand voice, factual accuracy, and editorial guidelines. This guide shows how to integrate Vex middleware for reliable, production-ready content generation.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with a React Server Component or API route using Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` or `streamText` to call LLMs like OpenAI or Anthropic. User prompts are enriched with brand guidelines and context, then streamed to the UI via Server-Sent Events. Post-generation, content may pass through fact-checking or SEO validation before display.

For complex flows, tool calls fetch real-time data (e.g., brand assets or fact sources), but failures like partial hallucinations or unvalidated tool results disrupt user trust. Edge functions handle inference, but timeouts occur on long content tasks. Vex middleware wraps SDK calls to verify outputs against custom rules before streaming.

This setup supports iterative refinement, where initial drafts are verified for tone/originality, then fact-checked via tools, ensuring compliance at every step.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Incomplete sentences or factual errors appear in real-time streams before full generation
risks.mitigation: Vex chunk-level verification blocks invalid tokens before UI render

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Fact-checking tools return unverified data directly to users
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool outputs against source schemas and accuracy thresholds

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs format brand-compliant content differently
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes outputs to consistent JSON schemas

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Long content generation + verification exceeds Vercel Edge limits
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification with streaming fallbacks prevents timeouts
